title: Your Roadmap Title
tagline: One punchy line shown on the dashboard card.
description: A paragraph shown at the top of the roadmap page. Explain who this is for and what they'll be able to do at the end. Keep it concrete.
outcome: The single sentence shown in the completion celebration — the tangible thing they can do once every task is checked off.

stages.title: First Stage Title
stages.summary: Two sentences describing what this stage is about and why it comes first.
objectives: Optional. A concrete thing the learner can do after this stage.
caseStudies: Optional. A real example the stage references, with one line of why it matters.
mistakes: Optional. A common mistake to avoid at this stage.

tasks.title: A reading task — title shows on the row
tasks.summary: One line shown under the task on the row and at the top of the detail panel.
tasks.whatYouGain: The concrete capability or insight you walk away with. Shown as the gold block at the top of the detail panel — write it to motivate.
details: Optional. Each string becomes a bullet under 'What to do' in the detail panel.

tasks.title: An exercise task with a linked resource
tasks.summary: Exercises are hands-on. Tell them what to produce.

resources.label: A book with multiple buy/read links

tasks.title: A project task with a checklist
tasks.summary: Projects are milestones — the deliverable that proves the stage stuck.
tasks.whatYouGain: The milestone artifact or confidence gained.
details: Describe the deliverable and how to know it's good.
checklist: Optional. Renders as a checklist card in the detail panel.
Use it for scorecards, criteria, or sub-steps.
These are visual only — they are not separately tracked.

stages.title: Second Stage Title
stages.summary: A minimal stage. Only id, index, title, duration, summary, and tasks are required.

tasks.title: A concept task — the minimal task shape
tasks.summary: Only id, title, type, and summary are required on a task. Everything else is optional.
